title: Vegetable oil supply per person vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: Average vegetable oil supply per capita, measured in kilograms per year versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Vegetable oil supply per person vs. GDP per capita

Average vegetable oil supply per capita, measured in kilograms per year versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This scatter plot maps the relationship between national wealth and vegetable oil consumption for 2023, with GDP per capita plotted on a logarithmic horizontal axis ranging from $2,000 to $100,000 and vegetable oil supply on a linear vertical axis from 0 to 35 kilograms per year. Countries are color-coded by region, including North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. The data points reveal a general positive correlation where higher GDP often aligns with higher oil supply, though significant variation exists. At the lower end of the economic spectrum, nations like the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Niger cluster below $3,000 GDP with oil supplies under 5 kg. In the middle range, countries such as Jordan, Belize, and Algeria show supplies between 10 and 20 kg with GDPs spanning roughly $10,000 to $20,000. Brazil stands out with a supply near 25 kg at a GDP around $18,000. Among high-income nations, Canada and the United States appear in the upper right with supplies exceeding 25 kg and GDPs over $50,000. Conversely, some wealthy European nations like Denmark and Norway display lower consumption levels, falling between 2 and 8 kg despite GDPs exceeding $60,000. The chart includes a diverse set of labeled nations from Guinea-Bissau and Honduras to Austria and Ireland, illustrating that while wealthier nations generally consume more vegetable oil, specific national averages vary widely within similar income brackets. All monetary values in the visualization are expressed in international dollars at 2021 prices.
